content.problemStatement:
Oh, these difficult contests... In each contest, there are so many hard tasks Chef cannot solve. However, he knows that he can only become a successful coder if he works hard. Fortunately, he has a problem in mind for training.

You are given two positive integers aaa and bbb. You may apply the following magical operation any number of times (including zero):


choose an arbitrary **positive** integer ddd
perform exactly one of the following actions:

add ddd to aaa, add d−1d-1d−1 to bbb
add d−1d-1d−1 to aaa, add ddd to bbb


Chef is asking you to find out if it is possible to obtain a=ba = ba=b. Help him!
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers aaa and bbb.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing the string `"YES"` if it is possible to obtain a=ba = ba=b or `"NO"` if it is impossible.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: One possible strategy for Chef is:

choose d=10d = 10d=10, add d−1=9d-1=9d−1=9 to aaa and d=10d=10d=10 to bbb; after this operation, a=14a = 14a=14 and b=17b = 17b=17
choose d=10d = 10d=10, add d=10d=10d=10 to aaa and d−1=9d-1=9d−1=9 to bbb; afterwards, a=24a = 24a=24 and b=26b = 26b=26
choose d=9d = 9d=9, add d=9d=9d=9 to aaa and d−1=8d-1=8d−1=8 to bbb; afterwards, a=33a = 33a=33 and b=34b = 34b=34
choose d=9d = 9d=9, add ddd to aaa and d−1d-1d−1 to bbb; finally, we have a=b=42a = b = 42a=b=42
